_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MCh/DNB equivalent in Plastic Surgery/ Reconstructive Surgery
clinical_indications: Injuries and traumatic amputations, life-threatening injuries
Replantation is only considered in a stable patient.

Indications for digital replantation include (1) amputated thumbs, (2) multiple digits, (3) single digit distal to the flexor digitorum superficialis tendon, and (4) all digital amputations in children.
Upper extremity amputations at the level of the wrist and hand should be replanted unless there are absolute life-threatening contraindications.

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with planned line of treatment and advice for admission.
Clinical photograph of the affected part

claim.label: Detailed indoor case papers with treatment information

common_queries: Doppler report missing at pre-authorization or claim stage.
MLC/FIR report (if traumatic) missing at pre-authorization or claim stage.
Clinical photograph of the affected part missing or unclear.
Detailed Clinical notes lacking planned line of treatment or advice for admission.
Detailed procedure/Operative notes missing or incomplete at claim stage.
Post-procedure clinical photograph missing at claim stage.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, examination.
Revascularization of damaged limbs/digits is technically feasible; Replantation refers to the reattachment and revascularization of an amputated part such as digit or hand.
Developments in microsurgery have led to excellent results in limb/digit replantation.
additional_information.fitness_criteria: Replantation is only considered in a stable patient.
additional_information.comorbidity_management: There are comorbid conditions taken into consideration, with the standard score from the Charlson comorbidity index as follows: 1 point (myocardial infarct, congestive heart failure, peripheral vascular disease, dementia, cerebrovascular disease, chronic lung disease, connective tissue disease, ulcer, chronic liver disease, diabetes), 2 points (hemiplegia, moderate or severe kidney disease, diabetes with end-organ damage, tumor, leukemia, lymphoma), 3 points (moderate or severe liver disease), 6 points (malignant tumor, metastasis, AIDS). Replantation success also depends on patient co-morbidities including smoking status, general health (psychiatric conditions), and coagulopathy.
contraindications: Absolute life-threatening contraindications (for upper extremity amputations)
